studyMetadata.title: Girma cikin Almasihu (Kashi na 1)
studyMetadata.subtitle: Darasi na almajirantawa
studyMetadata.description: Shin kana girma cikin Almasihu? Allah na son duk ƴaƴansa su yi girma a cikin Yesu. Idan ba ka girma, wannan binciken zai taimaka wajen baiyana menene matsala rashin girman kuma ya za a yi ƙoƙari daidaita al'amuran. Idan kuma ka na girma, wannan binciken zai taimaka wurin ƙara wajen yin girma. Kowane sashe na buƙata amfini da Littafi Mai Tsarki tare da mai karatu ya bincikin kansa ta wurin yin tunani mai zurfi.

elements.text1: Yaya Allah yake da gaske? Mutane suna da ra'ayoyi daban-daban — wasu suna ganin Shi kamar mai nisa da ba za a iya sanin Shi ba, wasu kuma kamar wata ƙarfi mai alheri, ko mai ba da dokokin duniya. Amma ba a bar mu ba mu yi hasashe. Allah Ya bayyana Kansa a gare mu a cikin Littafi Mai Tsarki, kuma a can ne muke fara.
elements.text2: No Allah wa'i goonga? Neɓe ngodi miijo'en ɗuɗɗum — ko'e ngoni on kaloo e neɗɗo mooftataake, ɗo'e ngoni on ko semmbe lobbo, walla ko jogorɗo hokkude sariya duuniyaaru. Ammaa min ngoodaaka nde min ngara miñe. Allah hollii hoyre maako e meeɗen nder Deftere Ceniiɗo, nden ɗoon tan ngoni warnde.
elements.text3: What is God really like? People have all sorts of ideas — some think of Him as distant and unknowable, others as a kind force, or a cosmic rule-giver. But we don't have to guess. God has revealed Himself to us in the Bible, and that is where we begin.

elements.text1: Allah waye?

elements.passageText5: <sup>3</sup>We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,

elements.question1: Mene ne wannan aya take faɗa mana game da wanda Allah yake?
elements.question2: Ɗume aya nde hollata'en e yonta Allah woni?
elements.question3: What does this verse tell us about who God is?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan aya tana ganin Allah a matsayin 'Uban Ubangijinmu Yesu Almasihu', wanda yake nuna cewa Allah ba wata ƙarfi mara rai ba, sai dai Wani mai rai wanda yake tsaye a cikin dangantakar Uba-da-Ɗa tare da Yesu. Yana kuma nuna cewa Allah wani ne da za mu iya gode Masa da kuma magana da Shi cikin addu'a.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Aya nde hollata Allah ko 'Nyaako Joomiraawo meeɗen Yeesu Almasiihu', nde hollata ko Allah waaɗaa semmbe mooftataake, ammaa ko neɗɗo goɗɗo woodi nder golle Baabiraawo-e-Ɓiɗɗo e Yeesu. Hollata kadi ko Allah neɗɗo min mbettoto e min ngettoo e do'aare.
elements.sampleAnswer3: This verse identifies God as 'the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ', revealing that God is not an impersonal force but a personal being who stands in a Father-Son relationship with Jesus. It also shows that God is someone to whom we can give thanks and speak to in prayer.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus yana amfani da wani takamaiman lakabi ga Allah a nan — mene ne wannan lakabi yake faɗa maka game da dangantakar da Allah yake da Yesu, kuma me za a iya kira wannan game da halinSa?
elements.questionHint2: Bulus waɗɗii innde goɗɗo e Allah ɗoo — ɗume innde nde hollata ma e jonta Allah woodi e Yeesu, nden ɗume ɗum hollata ma e yonta maako?
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a very specific title for God here — what does that title tell you about the kind of relationship God has with Jesus, and what might that imply about His nature?

elements.text1: Wace shaida ce ta nuna cewa Allah yana nan?
elements.text2: Ɗume hujja woodi nde hollata Allah woodi?
elements.text3: What evidence is there that God exists?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Dubi yadda sararin sama yake bayyana ɗaukakar Allah!
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>Sammai suna shelar

elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky displays his handiwork.

elements.question1: Mene ne wannan aya take nuna game da duniya ta halitta a matsayin shaida ta Allah?
elements.question2: Ɗume aya nde hollata e yonta duuniyaaru tagaaɗo ko hujja e Allah?
elements.question3: What does this verse suggest about the natural world as evidence for God?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan aya tana nuna cewa halitta kanta shaida ce ta wanzuwar Allah da ɗaukakarSa. Sama da sararin sama ba su tsaya tsaƙar ba — suna 'bayyana' da 'nuna' wani abu game da Allah. Kamar yadda wata zane-zane take nuna mai zane, haka duniya ta halitta take nuna Mahalicci. Shaida ga Allah ba a ɓoye take ba; tana bayyane a sama mana kowace rana.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Aya nde hollata ko tagaaɗo goɗɗo ko shaahid e goodnde Allah e teddungal maako. Leydi e samaa ngondaa seyɗuɓe — ngollata 'shelata' e 'hollude' ko Allah woni. Ko no njiɗdi goɗɗi hollata jiɗɗo naɗɗo ɗam, noon tagaaɗo hollata Tagiiɗo. Hujja e Allah waaɗaa ɓowri; hollita dow e meeɗen koo ɗume sahaa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: The verse suggests that creation itself is a witness to God's existence and glory. The sky and heavens are not neutral — they actively 'declare' and 'display' something about God. Just as a piece of artwork points to an artist, the created world points to a Creator. Evidence for God is not hidden away; it is visible above us every day.
elements.questionHint1: Idan aka ce sammai suna 'bayyana' wani abu, me hakan ke nuna game da abin da mai kallo a duniya ya halitta zai iya ƙarshe?
elements.questionHint2: Too samaa ngoni 'shelata' ko ɗume, ɗume ɗum hollata e ko neɗɗo yi'oowo duuniyaaru tagaaɗo foti waawa hasndude?
elements.questionHint3: If the heavens are said to 'declare' something, what does that imply about what an observer of the natural world should be able to conclude?

elements.passageText1: <sup>13</sup>Kai ne ka halicci kowace gaɓa ta jikina, <sup>14</sup>Ina yabonka gama kai abin tsoro ne,

elements.passageText5: <sup>13</sup>Certainly you made my mind and heart; you wove me together in my mother's womb. <sup>14</sup>I will give you thanks because your deeds are awesome and amazing. You knew me thoroughly;

elements.question1: Mene ne bayyanar da mawaki ya yi game da kasancewar an 'saƙa' shi tare take nuna game da shiga-hannun Allah a rayuwar ɗan Adam?
elements.question2: Ɗume hollata haala mawɗo jabura nde woni 'nanndinoowo' hollata e yonta Allah waɗɗi nder nguurndam ɓiɓɓe Aadama?
elements.question3: What does the psalmist's description of being 'woven together' suggest about God's involvement in human life?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Hoton kasancewar an 'saƙa' shi tare yana nuna cewa rayuwar ɗan Adam ba haɗari ba ce ko wani abin da ya faru ne kawai ta hanyar halittar jiki, sai dai wani aiki na gangan, mai kula, na ƙwarewa da Allah ya yi. Kamar yadda mai saƙa ke aiki da gangan, zare bayan zare, haka Allah ya kasance a cikin ƙirƙirar kowane mutum kai tsaye. Wannan yana nuna sani mai zurfi da kuma kulawa ta Allah ga kowane ɗan Adam tun farkon rayuwarsa.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Hoore 'nanndinoowo' nde hollata ko nguurndam ɓiɓɓe Aadama waaɗaa laaɓal walla golle biologi tan, ammaa ko golle goonɗinɗe tiiɗɗe, ɗiiɗe ɗe Allah waɗi. Ko no nanndinoowo golloraa e miñe, suudu faade suudu, noon Allah waɗɗii neɗɗo koo gooto goonga. Ɗum hollata anndal toowngal e mbaroodi Allah e neɗɗo koo gooto tuma futtaare mum.
elements.sampleAnswer3: The image of being 'woven together' suggests that human life is not an accident or a random biological event, but an intentional, careful act of craftsmanship by God. Just as a weaver works deliberately, thread by thread, God was personally involved in forming each person. This points to God's intimate knowledge of and care for every individual human being from the very beginning of their existence.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da abin da saƙa ke ƙunsa — za ka bayyana ta a matsayin aiki na sakaci, na gaɓoɗi, ko ta gangan kuma mai hankali? Mene ne hakan ke faɗa maka game da yadda Allah Ya ƙirƙira ka?
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e ko nannde yonkii — ko waɗa laawol ngol goonɗataɗo walla ko laawol ngol mo gaadoraaka? Ɗume ɗum hollata ma e yonta Allah tagi maa?
elements.questionHint3: Think about what weaving involves — would you describe it as a careless, random process, or a deliberate and careful one? What does that tell you about how God formed you?

elements.passageText1: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi waɗansu mu'ujizai da yawa dabam dabam a gaban almajiran, waɗanda ba a rubuta a littafin nan ba. <sup>31</sup>Amma an rubuta waɗannan ne, domin ku ba da gaskiya Yesu shi ne Almasihu, Ɗan Allah, ta wurin gaskatawa kuma ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText2: <sup>30</sup>Yesu ya yi alamun ban mamaki da yawa a idanun almajiransa waɗanda ba a rubuta a wannan littafi ba. <sup>31</sup>Amma an rubuta waɗannan ne, domin ku ba da gaskiya cewa Yesu shi ne Almasihu, Ɗan Allah. Saˈan nan kuma, ta wurin ba da gaskiya ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText3: <sup>30</sup>30 Yesu ya yi waɗansu abubuwan banmamaki da yawa a gaban almajiransa da ba a rubuta a wannan littafi ba. <sup>31</sup>31 An dai rubuta waɗannan ne don ku gaskata Yesu ne Kiristi, Ɗan Allah kuma ta wurin gaskatawa ku sami rai a cikin sunansa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>30</sup>30 Yeesu waɗii ko hayɗinii feere‑feere ɗuɗɗum yeeso tokkuɓemo ɗum windaaka nder ɗerewol ngo'ol. <sup>31</sup>31 Ammaa ɗu'um ɗum windaa ngam koolooɗon Yeesu woni Almasiihu Ɓiɗɗo Allah, nden laawol hoolaakimo keɓon yonki ki re'ataa barka inde maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>30</sup>Now Jesus performed many other miraculous signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not recorded in this book. <sup>31</sup>But these are recorded so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

elements.question1: Me ya sa Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta waɗannan alamomin? Mene ne hakan ke faɗa mana game da manufar Littafi Mai Tsarki?
elements.question2: Ngam ɗume Yaya wi'i o windii aayeeji ɗii? Ɗume ɗum hollata'en e yonta Deftere Ceniiɗo?
elements.question3: Why does John say he recorded these signs? What does this tell us about the purpose of the Bible?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya ce ya rubuta waɗannan alamomin ne domin masu karatu su zo su gaskata cewa Yesu shi ne Almasihu, Ɗan Allah, kuma ta hanyar wannan gaskatawa su sami rai a cikin sunansa. Wannan yana faɗa mana cewa Littafi Mai Tsarki ba tarihi kawai ba ne ko tarin koyarwar ɗabi'a — yana da wata manufa ta bayyana bisharar ceto. An rubuta shi don ya kawo mutane ga gaskata, kuma ta hanyar gaskatawa, ga rai.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya wi'i o windii aayeeji ɗii ngam koolooɓe ngara goonɗina Yeesu woni Almasiihu Ɓiɗɗo Allah, nden e laawol goonɗingal ngal keɓon yonki ki re'ataa barka e inɗe maako. Ɗum hollata'en ko Deftere Ceniiɗo waaɗaa tarihii tan walla deftere jannginirde ɗoyngal — e nder mum akitaare goonɗingande goonga. Windaama ngam naatnoyde neɓe e goonɗingal, nden e laawol goonɗingal, e yonki.
elements.sampleAnswer3: John says he recorded these signs so that readers would come to believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and through that belief find life in his name. This tells us that the Bible is not merely a historical record or a collection of moral teachings — it has an intentional, evangelistic purpose. It is written to bring people to faith and, through faith, to life.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna yana faɗa mana kai tsaye abin da yake fatan ga masu karatu — za ka gano burin biyu da ya ambata, kuma yaya suke da alaƙa da juna?
elements.questionHint2: Yaya hollitii goonga laawol ngol o yiɗi koolooɓe ngara nder — ana waawa hasnude kaɗe ɗiɗi o wi'i, nden no ɗe naɓɓi?
elements.questionHint3: John tells us directly what outcome he was hoping for in his readers — can you spot the two goals he mentions, and how are they connected to each other?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>A zamanin dā, Allah ya yi wa kakannin kakanninmu magana ta hanyoyi masu yawa iri iri, ta bakin annabawa, <sup>2</sup>amma a zamanin nan na ƙarshe, sai ya yi mana magana ta wurin Ɗansa, wanda ya sa magājin kome, wanda ta wurinsa ne kuma ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>Shi ne hasken ɗaukakar Allah, ainihin kamannin zatinsa kuma, shi kuma yana a riƙe da dukan abubuwa ta ikon faɗarsa. Bayan da ya tsarkake zunubanmu, sai ya zauna a dama da Maɗaukaki a can Sama.
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>A zamanin dā, Allah ya yi wa kakanninmu magana ta hanyoyi iri-iri da kuma a lokatai dabam-dabam ta wurin annabawa. <sup>2</sup>Amma a waɗannan kwanakin ƙarshe ya yi mana magana ta wurin Ɗansa. Ta wurin Ɗan ne Allah ya halicci kome da kome. Shi ne kuma ya zama magājin kome. <sup>3</sup>Shi ne hasken ɗaukakar Allah da kuma ainihin kamannin Allah. Daidai yake kuma da Allah. Yana riƙe da dukan kome ta wurin kalmarsa mai iko. Bayan ya tsabtace mu daga zunubanmu, sai ya zauna a sama a hannun daman Mai Ɗaukaka duka.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>1 A dā Allah ya yi wa kakanni-kakanninmu magana ta bakin annabawa a lokuta dabam-dabam da kuma a hanyoyi iri-iri, <sup>2</sup>2 amma a waɗannan kwanaki a ƙarshe ya yi magana da mu ta wurin Ɗansa, wanda ya naɗa magājin dukan abubuwa, wanda kuma ta wurinsa ya halicci duniya. <sup>3</sup>3 Ɗan shi ne hasken ɗaukakar Allah, shi ne kuma ainihin kamanninsa, yana riƙe da dukan abubuwa ta wurin kalmarsa mai iko. Bayan ya yi tsarkakewa don zunubai, sai ya zauna a hannun daman Maɗaukaki a sama.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>1 Ndenno Allah metanii maama'en meeɗen nde ɗuɗɗum e ɗate ɗuɗɗe daga konduɗe annabo'en. <sup>2</sup>2 Ammaa nder balɗe ɗe'e ɗe re'uki duuniyaaru o metiranii'en Ɓiɗɗo maako, mo o suɓi warta donoowo koo ɗume. Ɗonmaa barka maako Allah tagi duuniyaaru. <sup>3</sup>3 E mo holla jayngol teddungal Allah, nden nder koo ɗume e mo nandi e Allah. E mo jogorii koo ɗume wolde maako goodunde bawɗe. Gaɗa o waɗii laawol yaafaaki hakke, o jooɗii ton dow to jungo nyaamo ngo Allah Joomu Mangu.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>After God spoke long ago in various portions and in various ways to our ancestors through the prophets, <sup>2</sup>in these last days he has spoken to us in a son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he created the world. <sup>3</sup>The Son is the radiance of his glory and the representation of his essence, and he sustains all things by his powerful word, and so when he had accomplished cleansing for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Allah Ya yi magana da mu, a cewar wannan nassi? Mene ne take faɗa mana game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah?
elements.question2: No Allah metiri e meeɗen, e ko nassi nde wi'i? Ɗume ɗum hollata'en e jonta Yeesu woodi e Allah?
elements.question3: How has God spoken to us, according to this passage? What does it tell us about Jesus' relationship to God?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: A cewar wannan nassi, Allah ya yi magana a baya ta annabawa ta hanyoyi da yawa, amma yanzu Ya yi magana ta ƙarshe kuma ta cikakke ta wurin ɗanSa, Yesu. Wannan nassi yana da'awar abubuwa masu ban mamaki game da dangantakar Yesu da Allah: shi ne 'hasken ɗaukakarSa' da kuma 'ainihin kamanninSa', ma'ana ganin Yesu ganin ainihin yadda Allah yake. Yesu ya kuma halicci duniya, yana riƙe da dukan abubuwa, kuma ya cimma tsarkakewa daga zunubai — duk abubuwan da ke nuna mai ainihin halitta ta allahntaka.
elements.sampleAnswer2: E ko nassi nde wi'i, Allah metiri daga ɓooynde e laawol annabo'en e ɗate ɗuɗɗe, ammaa jooni o metiri e meeɗen goonga e Ɓiɗɗo maako Yeesu. Nassi nde wi'i ko huunde mawnde e yonta Yeesu e Allah: mo woni 'jayngol teddungal maako' e 'nanndinoowo yonta Allah', hollitii ko yi'ugol Yeesu ko yi'ugol Allah goonga. Yeesu tagi kadi duuniyaaru, jogorii koo ɗume, nden o waɗii laawol yaafaaki hakke — fuu hollita innde makko taƴunde e Allah.
elements.sampleAnswer3: According to this passage, God spoke in the past through the prophets in many different ways, but has now spoken definitively and finally through His Son, Jesus. The passage makes extraordinary claims about Jesus' relationship to God: he is the 'radiance of his glory' and the 'representation of his essence', meaning that to see Jesus is to see what God is truly like. Jesus also created the world, sustains all things, and has now accomplished the cleansing of sins — pointing to a unique divine identity.
elements.questionHint1: Wannan nassi yana bambance yadda Allah ya yi magana 'a zamanin dā' da yadda ya yi magana 'a zamanin nan na ƙarshe' — me ya bambanta tsakanin su biyu, kuma bayanin da aka yi na Ɗan a aya ta uku yana faɗa maka mene ne game da yadda Shi da Allah suke da alaƙa kai tsaye?
elements.questionHint2: Nassi nde waɗɗii tiitoonde goɗɗo no Allah metiri 'daga ɓooynde' e no o metiri 'nder balɗe ɗe re'uki' — ɗume yaawnii tawi ɗiɗi ɗii, nden ko ɗume haala Ɓiɗɗo e aya 3 nde hollata ma e yonta o naɓɓi e Allah goɗɗo?
elements.questionHint3: The passage contrasts how God spoke 'long ago' with how he has spoken 'in these last days' — what is different about the two ways, and what does the description of the Son in verse 3 tell you about how closely he is linked to God himself?

elements.question1: Mene ne ma'anar bayyan Allah a matsayin 'mai tsarki'? Me ya sa wannan kalma aka maimaita sau uku?
elements.question2: Ɗume hollata Allah wi'etee 'ceniiɗo'? Ngam ɗume wolde nde maccini tati?
elements.question3: What does it mean for God to be described as 'holy'? Why do you think that word is repeated three times?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Allah zama 'mai tsarki' yana nufin Shi ya keɓe gaba ɗaya — cikakke a dabi'arSa, tsattsarka, kuma ya bambanta gaba ɗaya da duk abin da yake a cikin duniyar halitta. Tsarki ba wata dabi'a ce ɗaya kawai ba a cikin dabi'u da yawa, sai dai yana magana game da bambanceSa na gaba ɗaya da ɗaukakarSa. Maimaitar kalmar sau uku ('mai tsarki, mai tsarki, mai tsarki') ita ce mafi ƙarfin jaddadawa a cikin Ibrananci — ba kawai cewa Allah mai tsarki ne sosai ba, sai dai cewa tsarki yana bayyana halinSa na ainihi zuwa mafi girman mataki da ya yiwu. Babu wata sifa ta Allah da aka yabi ta yadda aka ninka ta sau uku a Littafi Mai Tsarki.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Allah wi'etee 'ceniiɗo' hollata ko O taƴii gaa wala — ceniingal e yonta maako goonga, O laaɓi, nden O nandataa e ko woni nder duuniyaaru tagaaɗo. Ceniingal waaɗaa ko baɗal gootal e baɗe ɗuɗɗe, ammaa hollata ko O nandi e ko goɗɗum fuu. Maccina tati ('ceniiɗo, ceniiɗo, ceniiɗo') ko tiiɗgol toowngol nder Ibraniire — waaɗaa wi'ugol tan ko Allah ceniingal toowri, ammaa ko ceniingal hollata yonta maako goonga faa daraaja toownde fuu. Baɗal gootal Allah waaɗaa yaabiramaa e laawol maccina tati noon e Deftere Ceniiɗo.
elements.sampleAnswer3: For God to be 'holy' means He is utterly set apart — morally perfect, pure, and completely unlike anything in the created world. Holiness is not just one quality among many but speaks to His absolute otherness and majesty. The threefold repetition ('holy, holy, holy') is the strongest form of emphasis in Hebrew — it is not just saying God is very holy, but that holiness defines His very being to the highest possible degree. No other attribute of God is praised in this tripled way in Scripture.
elements.questionHint1: A cikin harshen yau da kullun, idan kana son jaddada yadda wani abu yake gaskiya sosai, me za ka yi — kuma yaya wannan ra'ayi yake iya amfani don bayyana dalilin da ya sa mala'iku suka maimaita kalmar 'mai tsarki' sau uku maimakon sau ɗaya?
elements.questionHint2: Nder haala hannde, too a yiɗi hollitaade no huunde nde tiiɗi, ɗume a waɗata — nden no miijo ngoo waɗɗira ngam hollitaade ngam ɗume mala'ika'en maccini wolde 'ceniiɗo' tati, walla go'o tan?
elements.questionHint3: In everyday language, if you want to stress how extremely something is true, what do you do — and how might that same idea apply to why the angels repeat the word 'holy' three times rather than just once?

elements.passageText2: <sup>7</sup>Da kun san ni, da kun san Ubana ma. Amma daga yanzu kun san shi, kun kuma gan shi.
Ya Ubangiji, in ka nuna mana Uban, to, ai, bukatarmu ta biya ke nan.

Ubangiji, ka nuna mana Uban, wannan kuwa zai biya bukatarmu.
Ba san ni ba, Filibus, duk da daɗewan nan da na yi tare da ku? Duk wanda ya gan ni, ai, ya ga Uban. Yaya za ka ce, 'Ka nuna mana Uban'?

And from now on you do know him and have seen him.' <sup>8</sup>Philip said to him, 'Lord, show us the Father, and we will be content.' <sup>9</sup>Jesus replied, 'Have I been with you for so long, and you have not known me, Philip? elements.question1: Mene ne Yesu yake da'awa game da dangantakar da yake da Uba? Mene ne hakan ke nufin fahimtarmu game da Allah?
elements.question2: Ɗume Yeesu wi'i e yonta jonta maako e Nyaako Mo Dow? Ɗume ɗum hollata'en e yonta min goonɗinta Allah?
elements.question3: What does Jesus claim about the relationship between Himself and the Father? What does that mean for how we understand God?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu yana da'awa mai ban mamaki: cewa ganin Shi ganin Uba ne. Ba kawai mai isar da saƙo daga wurin Allah ba ne, ko wanda ke koyar da Allah — Shi ne ainihin surar Allah da kasancewansa. Wannan yana nufin cewa idan muke son sanin yadda Allah yake, ba mu buƙatar neman wani wuri banda Yesu: tausayinSa, gaskiyarSa, mu'amalarSa da mutane, son da Yake da shi na ba da ransa — duk waɗannan suna bayyana halin Allah Uba.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yeesu wi'i ko huunde mawnde: ko yi'ugol Muuɗum ko yi'ugol Nyaako Mo Dow. O waaɗaa tan ko jiilɗo haala Allah, walla ko jiilɗo jannginirde e Allah — O woni goonga hoyre e janngorde Allah. Ɗum hollata too min yiɗi anndude no Allah wa'i, min ngondataa miñe ka goɗɗum saka Yeesu: baroodi maako, goonga maako, no o golloraa e neɓe, sooynaare maako warta dow yonki maako — fuu hollata yonta Allah Nyaako Mo Dow.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus makes a remarkable claim: that to have seen Him is to have seen the Father. He is not merely a messenger from God, or someone who teaches about God — He is the very image and presence of God. This means that if we want to know what God is like, we do not need to look further than Jesus: His compassion, His honesty, His treatment of people, His willingness to lay down His life — all of these reveal the character of God the Father.
elements.questionHint1: Filibus yana roƙon a nuna masa Uban kamar dai Uba yana wani wuri dabam — me ya sa Yesu ya sami wannan tambayar abin mamaki, kuma abin da amsarSa take nuna game da inda ainihin ana iya samun Uba?
elements.questionHint2: Filibus daɗɗiinii hollugol Nyaako Mo Dow ko no o woni e wowru goɗɗo — ngam ɗume Yeesu mawnini tambunde nde, nden ɗume jaabawol maako hollita e to Nyaako Mo Dow waawi heɓeede goonga?
elements.questionHint3: Philip asks to be shown the Father as if the Father were somewhere else — why does Jesus find this question so surprising, and what does His answer imply about where the Father can actually be found?

elements.question1: Mene ne wannan nassi take faɗa mana game da dangantakar Allah da lokaci?
elements.question2: Ɗume nassi nde hollata'en e yonta Allah woodi e sahaa?
elements.question3: What does this passage tell us about God's relationship to time?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan nassi yana faɗa mana cewa Allah yana wanzuwa a wajen lokaci kuma a gabansa — Ya kasance Allah kafin ƙasa ko tuddai su wanzu, kuma zai ci gaba da zama Allah har abada. Aya ta huɗu tana nuna cewa Allah baya fuskantar lokaci yadda muke fuskantu: abin da yake ji na shekara dubu a gare mu yana zama kamar kwana ɗaya a gare Shi. Allah ba ya cinyewa da lokaci yadda muke cinyewa; Yana ganin dukan tarihi a gabansa a zamanin yau, ma'ana ba zai taɓa kama Shi ba a banza, ba ya yi gaggawa, kuma ba ya jinkin a bayarSa.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Nassi nde hollata'en ko Allah woni ka jowre sahaa — O woni Allah ado leydi walla dutsi ngoni, nden O wona Allah faa abada. Aya 4 hollata ko Allah anndaa sahaa ko no meeɗen anndata: ko meeɗen gannoo ko duuɓi ujunere ko no hannde mawnde nde ɓenniri e yeeso maako. Allah jogorataa sahaa ko no meeɗen jogoratnoo; O yi'a tarihii fuu nder jooni, ma'ana huunde walaa nde naawa mo, O naawaa, nden O waddetaako.
elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God exists outside of and before time itself — He was God before the earth or mountains existed, and He will continue to be God forever. Verse 4 shows that God does not experience time as we do: what feels like a thousand years to us is like a single day to Him. God is not constrained by time the way we are; He sees all of history as present to Him at once, which means He is never caught off guard, never in a hurry, and never late.
elements.questionHint1: Mawaki yana cewa Allah ya wanzu kafin tuddai ko ƙasa su 'hafu' — mene ne hakan ke faɗa maka game da ko Allah yana da farko, kuma yaya hakan zai iya shafar yadda Yake fuskantar lokaci idan aka kwatanta da mu?
elements.questionHint2: Mawɗo jabura wi'i Allah woni ka ado dutsi walla leydi 'futtii' — ɗume ɗum hollata ma e ko Allah heɓi fuɗɗorde, nden no ɗum waɗɗira e yonta O gannoo sahaa tawi O nandi e meeɗen?
elements.questionHint3: The psalmist says God existed even before the mountains or the earth were 'born' — what does that tell you about whether God had a beginning, and how might that affect the way He experiences time compared to us?

elements.question1: Mene ne ma'anar cewa 'babu sauyawa ko wata alamar sakewa' a cikin Allah? Me ya sa hakan yana da muhimmanci?
elements.question2: Ɗume hollata 'walaa waylude walla kaɗal nde ko ɗume' e Allah? Ngam ɗume ɗum mawnii?
elements.question3: What does it mean that there is 'no variation or the slightest hint of change' in God? Why is that significant?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yana nufin cewa halin Allah, ƙaunarSa, alkawarirSa, da nagartarSa ba su canza ko su karkace. Ba kamar dangantakar ɗan Adam ba — inda mutane za su iya zama masu kirki wata rana kuma su yi sanyi a wata — Allah yana da jituwa ta cikakke. Wannan yana da muhimmanci sosai saboda yana nufin za mu iya amincewa da Shi gaba ɗaya. ƘaunarSa a gare mu yau tana da ƙarfi ɗaya kamar yadda take jiya kuma yadda za ta kasance a gobe. Alkawarinsa ba ya ƙarewa ko a sake shi. Ba ma buƙatar damuwa cewa Allah Ya canza ransa game da mu.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Ɗum hollata ko yonta Allah, yidde maako, alkawale makko, e lobbo maako waylataa walla yokkitaa. Ko no nanondiranke ɓiɓɓe Aadama walaa noon — ɗo neɓe ngona lobɓe sahaa gooto nden ngona kaɓɓiɗɓe gooto goɗɗo — Allah goonga e jonta maako fuu. Ɗum mawnii sosaa ngam hollita min ngona ngondita mo gaa wala. Yidde maako e meeɗen hannde tiiɗi ko no tiiɗiri hanki nden no tiiɗirta jango. Alkawale makko waddetaako walla yoɓetaaka. Min ngonataa e miñe min njaɓɓata Allah waylii miijo maako e meeɗen.
elements.sampleAnswer3: It means that God's character, His love, His promises, and His goodness never shift or fluctuate. Unlike human relationships — where people can be kind one day and cold the next — God is completely consistent. This is enormously significant because it means we can trust Him fully. His love for us today is exactly as strong as it was yesterday and will be tomorrow. His promises do not expire or get revised. We never have to worry that God has changed His mind about us.
elements.questionHint1: Yi tunani game da lokaci da canjin yanayin wani ko karye alkawari ya sa da wahala a amince da shi — yaya Allah kasancewa mai ɗan canzawa gaba ɗaya zai iya bambanta yadda kake danganta da Shi?
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e sahaa nde waylugol yonta neɗɗo walla alkawale karɓaɗe waɗi ko waawi goonɗingal tiiɗungal — no Allah waylitataaka fuu waɗɗira e yonta a naɓɓira e muuɗum?
elements.questionHint3: Think about a time when someone's changing moods or broken promises made it hard to trust them — how might God being completely unchanging make a difference to how you relate to Him?

elements.question1: Mene ne wannan nassi take faɗa mana game da kasancewar Allah? Shin wannan tunani ne mai ta'aziyya ko mai ƙalubale a gare ka?
elements.question2: Ɗume nassi nde hollata'en e yonta Allah woodi fuu? Ko ɗume jooy nde hollita ma daliiji walla ko ko waɗi fure maa?
elements.question3: What does this passage tell us about God's presence? Is that a comforting or a challenging thought for you?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Wannan nassi yana faɗa mana cewa Allah yana ko'ina — a zahiri babu wani wuri a duniya, daga tsayin sama zuwa zuriyar Sheol, inda Allah yake rashi. Kasancewarsa ba ta yiwuwar guduwa. Ko wannan ta'aziyya ce ko ƙalubale mai yiwuwa ya dogara da yadda muke ji game da Allah. Ga wanda yake amincewa da Allah kuma yana matuƙar son taimakonSa, na'am mai ta'aziyya ce sosai — ko nawa mun ji nisa ko kaɗaici, hannunSa yana jagorantar mu. Amma yana iya zama ƙalubale ko abin bayyanawa, saboda yana nufin babu abin da ke cikin rayuwarmu da yake ɓoye daga gare Shi.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Nassi nde hollata'en ko Allah woni faa wowru fuu — ko goonga walaa ɗo e duuniyaaru, daga dow samaa faa ɓolol Sheol, Allah waaɗaa ɗoon. Goodnde maako yaltataake. Ko daliiji walla ko waɗi fure mai yiwi naɓɓirde no min anndata Allah. E muɗum goonɗiniima Allah nden yiɗirta ballal maako, ko daliiji tiiɗi sosaa — koo no min ganoo min ŋakki walla min kaɗaaki, jungo maako jogorii'en. Ammaa waawi kadi warde ko waɗi fure walla ko hollitee, ngam ɗum hollata ko walaa ko e nguurndam meeɗen ɓuuɓi ka yeeso maako.
elements.sampleAnswer3: This passage tells us that God is omnipresent — there is literally nowhere in the universe, from the heights of heaven to the depths of Sheol, where God is absent. His presence is inescapable. Whether that is comforting or challenging likely depends on how we feel about God. For someone who trusts God and longs for His help, it is deeply comforting — no matter how far or alone we feel, His hand is still guiding us. But it can also feel challenging or exposing, because it means nothing in our lives is hidden from Him.
elements.questionHint1: Mawaki yana lissafa abubuwan da suka sa'ake — sama da Sheol, kusa da nisa — don ya bayyana wani batu game da kasancewar Allah. Wane ƙarshe ne yake gina, kuma shin sanin Allah yana nan koyaushe yana jin daɗin labari mai kyau ko gaskiya mai damuwa a gare ka kai tsaye?
elements.questionHint2: Mawɗo jabura waɗɗii ɗiɗe nde naɓɓaani — samaa e Sheol, ɓuri ɓuri — ngam hollitaade ko Allah woni faa wowru fuu. Ko jaɓɓol bonde o mari, nden ko goodnde Allah koo ɗo woni, ko daliiji maa walla ko ko waɗi maa fure tuma maa faa hoyre maa?
elements.questionHint3: The psalmist lists extreme opposites — heaven and Sheol, near and far — to make a point about God's presence. What conclusion is he building towards, and does knowing God is always present feel like good news or an uncomfortable truth to you personally?

elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>The person who does not love does not know God, because God is love.

elements.question1: Yohanna ya ce 'Allah shi ne ƙauna' — ba kawai cewa Allah yana ƙauna ba, sai dai ƙauna tana bayyana wanda Yake. Mene ne bambancin da hakan ke yi?
elements.question2: Yaya wi'i 'Allah o yidde' — waaɗaa tan ko Allah yiddirta, ammaa ko yidde hollita yonta maako. Ɗume bambam ngam waɗɗata?
elements.question3: John says 'God is love' — not just that God loves, but that love defines who He is. What difference does that make?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Cewa Allah yana ƙauna yana cewa wani abin da Yake yi — wani aiki da zai iya zaɓar ko ya hana. Amma cewa Allah shi ne ƙauna yana nufin ƙauna ba ta rabuwa da ainihin kasancewarsa; ba zai iya dakatar da ƙauna ba fiye da yadda zai iya dakatar da zama Allah. Yana nufin ƙaunarSa ba amsa ce ga mu kasancewa masu cancancewa ba — tana gudana daga wanda Yake. Wannan yana nufin kuma cewa ƙauna ba kawai ji ne da Allah yake da shi ba, sai dai asalin bayyanarSa, wanda yake ba mu dalili don amincewa cewa ƙaunarSa ta dumu, ba tare da sharadi ba, kuma ba ta da iyaka.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Wi'ugol Allah yiddirta woni ko golle O waɗata — golle O waɗata walla yokkita. Ammaa wi'ugol Allah o yidde hollita ko yidde taƴii e yonta maako; O waawaa haɓɓude yidde ko no O waawiri haɓɓude wonde Allah. Ɗum hollata ko yidde maako waaɗaa jaabawol e meeɗen ngoni lobɓe — ɗum ƴiwoyta daga yonta maako. Ɗum hollata kadi ko yidde waaɗaa tan ko miijaango Allah woni, ammaa ko asali yonta maako, nde hokkata'en sababu ngam goonɗingal yidde maako tiiɗi, nde ɓuurtaa e sariya, nde re'ataa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Saying God loves is saying something He does — an action He can choose or withhold. But saying God is love means love is inseparable from His very being; He cannot stop being loving any more than He can stop being God. It means His love is not a response to us being lovable — it flows from who He is. This also means that love is not just a feeling God has but the core of His identity, which gives us grounds to trust that His love is permanent, unconditional, and inexhaustible.
elements.questionHint1: Yi la'akari da bambancin tsakanin cewa 'malami mai kirki ne' da 'malami wani lokaci yana nuna kirki' — wanne ke faɗar fiye game da ainihin bayyanarsa, kuma yaya wannan bambanci yake amfani da abin da Yohanna ke da'awa game da Allah?
elements.questionHint2: Miijo e bambam tawi wi'ugol 'janngirɗo ko lobbo' e wi'ugol 'janngirɗo ko lobbiri ko ɗume' — homo wi'irde fuu hollita yonta maako goonga, nden no bambam ngam naɓɓira e ko Yaya wi'i e Allah?
elements.questionHint3: Consider the difference between saying 'a teacher is kind' and 'a teacher sometimes acts kindly' — which says more about their core identity, and how does that distinction apply to what John is claiming about God?

elements.passageText2: <sup>8</sup>Amma Allah ya tabbatar mana yawan ƙaunarsa, da yake tun muna masu zunubi, Almasihu ya mutu saboda mu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>8</sup>8 Amma Allah ya nuna mana ƙaunarsa a wannan. Tun muna masu zunubi tukuna, Kiristi ya mutu dominmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>8</sup>8 Ammaa laawol ngol Allah hollii'en yidde maako woni, carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke, Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>But God demonstrates his own love for us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Bulus yake cewa Allah Ya tabbatar da ƙaunarSa? Mene ne abin mamaki game da lokacin — 'tun muna masu zunubi'?
elements.question2: No Bulus wi'i Allah hollii yidde maako? Ɗume mawnii tuma nde — 'carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke'?
elements.question3: How does Paul say God has demonstrated His love? What is striking about the timing — 'while we were still sinners'?

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya ce Allah Ya tabbatar da ƙaunarSa ta wurin mutuwar Almasihu a madadinmu. Abin da ya fi ban mamaki shi ne lokacin: Allah bai jira mu mu gyara rayuwarmu ba, mu sami yabo daga gare Shi, ko mu zama masu cancancewa da ƙaunarSa. Almasihu ya mutu dominmu tun muna masu zunubi — tun muna cikin tawaye ga Allah. Wannan yana juya zato na ɗan Adam cewa ƙauna dole a cancance ta. Ƙaunar Allah ba ladakin ɗabi'ar kirki ba ne; ita ce ƙauna ta 'yanci, ta farawa da aka bai wa waɗanda ba su yi komai da zai sa a cancance su.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Bulus wi'i Allah hollii yidde maako e maayugol Almasiihu ngam meeɗen. Ko mawnii tiiɗi ko tuma nde: Allah jaɓɓataako meeɗen min gyaa nguurndam meeɗen, min keɓa barke maako, walla min warda min cancanta yidde maako. Almasiihu maayii ngam meeɗen carel ko ngonɗen nder hakke — ko tuma ngonɗen nder taalugal e Allah. Ɗum yoɓa miijo ɓiɓɓe Aadama nde yidde foti cancintaa. Yidde Allah waaɗaa saraare e wallifaade nawe; ko yidde nde yaltii e yonta maako, hokkaaɗe e ɓe ngaɗaay ɗume fuu ngam cancinta ɗam.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says God demonstrated His love through the death of Christ on our behalf. What is especially striking is the timing: God did not wait for us to clean up our lives, earn His favour, or become worthy of His love. Christ died for us while we were still sinners — still in rebellion against God. This overturns the human assumption that love must be deserved. God's love is not a reward for good behaviour; it is a free, initiating love given to those who had done nothing to merit it.
elements.questionHint1: A cikin dangantakar ɗan Adam da yawa, muna nuna kirki ga waɗanda suka nuna mana kirki — don haka mene ne abin mamaki ko abin da ba a zata game da takamaiman lokaci da Bulus ya ce Allah Ya zaɓa don nuna ƙaunarSa?
elements.questionHint2: Nder nanondiranke ɓiɓɓe Aadama, min ngoɗɗitoo lobɓe e ɓe ngodɗitii'en lobɓe — ngam ɗume tuma nde Bulus wi'i Allah suɓi hollitaade yidde maako woni ɗume mawnii walla aɓii?
elements.questionHint3: In most human relationships, we tend to show kindness to people who have been kind to us — so what is surprising or unexpected about the specific moment Paul says God chose to demonstrate His love?

elements.passageText1: <sup>9</sup>Ta haka aka bayyana ƙaunar Allah gare mu, da Allah ya aiko da Ɗansa makaɗaici a duniya, domin mu sami rai madawwami ta wurinsa. <sup>10</sup>Ta haka ƙauna take, wato, ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu, ya aiko Ɗansa, hadayar sulhu saboda a gafarta zunubanmu.
elements.passageText2: <sup>9</sup>Ga yadda Allah ya nuna mana ƙaunarsa. Ya aiko da makaɗaicin Ɗansa zuwa cikin duniya domin mu sami rai ta wurinsa. <sup>10</sup>Ga yadda ƙauna ta gaskiya take, wato ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu, har ya aiko da Ɗansa domin yă zama hadaya ta ɗaukar alhakin zunubanmu.
elements.passageText3: <sup>9</sup>9 Ga yadda Allah ya nuna ƙaunarsa a cikinmu, Allah ya aiki makaɗaici Ɗansa zuwa duniya domin mu rayu ta wurinsa. <sup>10</sup>10 Wannan ita ce ƙauna, wato, ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu, ya kuma aiko Ɗansa yă zama hadaya ta kafara saboda zunubanmu.
elements.passageText4: <sup>9</sup>9 Allah hollii'en yidde muuɗum nde o liloyi Ɓiɗɗo maako go'oto tan mo o woodi nder duuniyaaru, ngam keɓen yonki ki re'ataa barka Ɓiɗɗo on. <sup>10</sup>10 Raa no yidde nde goonga wa'i, naa' enen ngiɗi Allah, ammaa Allah yiɗi'en o liloyi Ɓiɗɗo maako ngam warta cakkiri yaafaaki hakke meeɗen.
elements.passageText5: <sup>9</sup>By this the love of God is revealed in us: that God has sent his one and only Son into the world so that we may live through him. <sup>10</sup>In this is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.

elements.question1: A cewar Yohanna, wane ne ya ɗauki farkon wannan dangantaka — Allah ko mu? Mene ne hakan ke faɗa mana game da halin ƙaunar Allah?
elements.question2: E ko Yaya wi'i, homo suɓi fuɗɗude nanondiranke ngoo — Allah walla meeɗen? Ɗume ɗum hollata'en e yonta yidde Allah?
elements.question3: According to John, who took the initiative in this relationship — God or us? What does that tell us about the nature of God's love?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna yana bayyane cewa Allah ya ɗauki farkon — an bayyana ƙauna a nan ba a matsayin ƙaunarmu ga Allah ba, sai dai ƙaunarSa gare mu. Allah Ya tafi farko. Ya aiko da ɗanSa kafin mu neme Shi, don magance zunubin da ya raba mu da Shi. Wannan yana faɗa mana cewa ƙaunar Allah ba mai ta'amuli ba ne ko mai sharadi — ta fito daga cikinta kuma ba tare da sharadi ba. Ba mu ja ƙaunar Allah daga gare Shi ta hanyar ibada tamu ba; Ya ƙaunace mu a 'yanci kuma Ya aiwatar da wannan ƙauna da tsada mai yawa, yana aiko da kaɗaicin ɗanSa a matsayin hadaya.
elements.sampleAnswer2: Yaya hollitii goonga ko Allah suɓii fuɗɗude — yidde bayyanetaa ɗoo waaɗaa ko yidde meeɗen e Allah, ammaa ko yidde maako e meeɗen. Allah yahi fuɗɗude. O liloyi Ɓiɗɗo maako ado meeɗen miñi mo, ngam tiindude hakke nde yoɓtinii meeɗen e muuɗum. Ɗum hollata'en ko yidde Allah waaɗaa jaabawol walla nde naɓɓiri e sariya — ɗum yaltii e yonta maako e laawol nde ɓuurtooka. Min ngaannataa yidde Allah e laawol koolugol meeɗen; O yiɗi'en e nuunɗugol nden o waɗi laawol ngol e deeɗal mawnwal, o liloyi go'oto Ɓiɗɗo maako ko cakkiri.
elements.sampleAnswer3: John is explicit that God took the initiative — love is defined here not as our love for God, but as His love for us. God moved first. He sent His Son before we sought Him, to deal with the sin that separated us from Him. This tells us that God's love is not reactive or conditional — it is originating and unconditional. We do not draw God's love out of Him by our devotion; He loved us freely and acted on that love at great cost, sending His only Son as a sacrifice.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce 'ba mu ne muka ƙaunaci Allah ba, sai dai shi ne ya ƙaunace mu' — wane ya ce Ya fara tafiya, kuma mene ne bambancin da mai yiwuwa ya yi maka kai tsaye sanin cewa dangantakar ta fara da Allah, ba da kai ba?
elements.questionHint2: Yaya wi'i 'naa' enen ngiɗi Allah, ammaa Allah yiɗi'en' — homo o wi'i fuɗɗii, nden ɗume bambam ngam waɗɗata e maa faa hoyre maa anndude ko nanondiranke ngoo fuɗɗii e Allah, waaɗaa e maa?
elements.questionHint3: John says 'not that we have loved God, but that he loved us' — who does he say made the first move, and what practical difference might it make to you personally to know that the relationship started with God, not with you?

chapters.chapterTitle3: Jesus Christ, the Son of God

elements.text3: Many people think of Jesus as a great teacher or a good man who lived two thousand years ago. But the Bible presents Someone far greater. Surprisingly, Jesus did not begin at Bethlehem — He has always existed. In this study we look at who Jesus really is.
What do we know about Jesus before He came to earth?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil'azal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne tun fil'azal yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Dukan abubuwa sun kasance ta gare shi ne, ba kuma abin da ya kasance na abubuwan da suka kasance, sai ta game da shi.
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>Tun farko akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne wanda tun farko yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurinsa ne aka yi dukan abubuwa, kuma babu wani abu a cikin dukan halittar da aka yi wanda ba tare da shi aka yi ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>1 Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>2 Yana nan tare da Allah tun farar farawa. <sup>3</sup>3 Ta wurin Kalman ne aka halicci dukan abubuwa. In ba tare da shi ba, babu abin da aka yi wanda aka yi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>1 Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi'eteeɗo Wolde e ɗon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah. <sup>2</sup>2 Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi'eteeɗo Wolde on e wondi e Allah. <sup>3</sup>3 O tagii koo ɗume. Walaa goɗɗum nder fuu ko tagaa ɗum um tagiraayi laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
elements.note: In this passage the ’Word’ is referring to Jesus.

elements.question3: John calls Jesus ’the Word’. What does this passage tell us about where Jesus was before He came to earth?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer3: Before coming to earth, Jesus — the Word — already existed. He was with God in the beginning, meaning He has always been in close relationship with the Father. More than that, He was fully God Himself. John also tells us that all things were created through Him, meaning Jesus was not a created being who appeared at some point in history, but the eternal Creator who was present before anything else existed.
elements.questionHint3: John says the Word was ’in the beginning’ — does that suggest Jesus had a starting point, or that He was already there before anything began? What else does John say about His role before creation?

elements.passageText1: <sup>15</sup>Shi ne surar Allah marar ganuwa, magāji ne tun ba a halicci kome ba, <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ƙasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake riƙe da su.
elements.passageText2: <sup>15</sup>Almasihu shi ne kamannin Allah wanda ba a iya gani, shi ne Ɗan fari gaban dukan halitta. <sup>16</sup>Gama ta wurinsa ne aka yi kome da kome, abubuwan da suke a sammai da kuma a nan duniya, da abubuwan da ake gani da waɗanda ba a iya gani, ko kujerun mulki, ko mulkoki, ko masu sarauta, ko ikoki. Dukan abubuwan da aka halitta, an halicce su ne ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa. <sup>17</sup>Almasihu shi ne a gaba da dukan kome, ta wurinsa ne kuma dukan halitta ta kasance a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText3: <sup>15</sup>15 Kiristi shi ne surar da ake gani na Allah wanda ba a iya gani, ɗan fari a kan dukan halitta. <sup>16</sup>16 Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, waɗanda ake gani, da waɗanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>17 Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>15</sup>15 Ngikku Almasiihu mo ngi'eten ɗum go'o e ngikku Allah mo yi'ataake. E mo ɗon ko Allah taga koo ɗume, o mawɗo dow koo ɗume ko tagaa. <sup>16</sup>16 Barka maako Allah tagi koo ɗume gonɗum dow e ley, ko yi'etee e ko yi'ataake, hawti e malaa'ika'en, e ginni, e koo ɗume gooduɗum laamu e bawɗe. Koo ɗume kanko tagiɗum, nden ngam maako koo ɗume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>17 Almasiihu e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee, barka maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities—all things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.question3: What does Paul say Jesus’ role was in creation? What does verse 17 suggest about His ongoing relationship to the universe?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that all things — in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible — were created by Jesus, through Jesus, and for Jesus. His role in creation was total: nothing came into existence apart from Him. Verse 17 then goes further, saying that in Him all things ’hold together’. This means Jesus is not just the originator of creation but its ongoing sustainer — the universe continues to exist and cohere because of Him. He is not a distant figure who started things and stepped away; He is actively involved in holding everything together at this very moment.
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses three prepositions in verse 16 — all things were created ’by’, ‘through’, and ’for’ Jesus. What does adding those three together suggest about the scope of His involvement in creation, and how does verse 17 extend that idea into the present?

elements.text3: Why was He called Jesus?

elements.question3: What does the name ’Jesus’ mean, and what does that tell us about the purpose of His coming?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer3: The angel explains the name directly: Jesus means ’he will save his people from their sins’. The name itself is a mission statement. Jesus did not come primarily as a political liberator, a moral teacher, or a miracle worker — He came to deal with the problem of sin. His name tells us that rescue from sin is the central purpose of His life, and that this rescue was planned and announced before He was even born.
elements.questionHint3: The angel gives a reason for the name with the word ’for’ — what specific problem does He say Jesus will come to solve, and does that surprise you given what people might have expected a Saviour to do?

elements.text3: How do we know that Jesus is truly human?

elements.passageText1: <sup>7</sup>Sai ta haifi ɗanta na fari, ta rufi shi da zanen goyo, ta kuma kwantar da shi a wani komi dabbobi, don ba su sami ɗaki a masaukin ba. ... <sup>40</sup>Ɗan yaron kuwa ya girma, ya kawo ƙarfi, yana mai matuƙar hikima. Alherin Allah kuwa yana tare da shi.
elements.passageText2: <sup>7</sup>Ta haifi ɗanta na fari, ta rufe shi da zanen goyo, ta kuma kwantar da shi a abin da ake ba dabbobi abinci a ciki, domin ba su sami ɗaki a masauki ba. ... <sup>40</sup>Yaron ya yi girma, ya kuma yi ƙarfi, yana cike da hikima. Alherin Allah kuma yana tare da shi.
elements.passageText3: <sup>7</sup>7 ta kuwa haifi ɗanta na fari. Ta nannaɗe shi a zane ta kuma kwantar da shi cikin wani kwami, don ba su sami ɗaki a masauƙi ba. <sup>40</sup>40 Yaron kuwa ya yi girma ya yi ƙarfi; ya cika da hikima, alherin Allah kuwa yana bisansa.
elements.passageText4: <sup>7</sup>7 Sey o danyi ɓiɗɗo gorko, afo maako, o saawimo nder wudere wooɗunde, o fukkinimo nder to ɗum waɗanta marle nyamndu, ngam walaa njayri nder suudu hoɓɓe. <sup>40</sup>40 Yeesu mawni, sembiɗi, faamu heewimo, nden barka Allah e dow maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>7</sup>And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in strips of cloth and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn. ... <sup>40</sup>The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom, and the favor of God was upon him.

elements.question3: What details in these verses show that Jesus experienced a genuinely human childhood?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer3: Verse 7 shows that Jesus was born in the ordinary, physical way — wrapped in cloth like any newborn infant and laid in a manger. He entered the world in vulnerability and poverty, not in heavenly splendour. Verse 40 then tells us He grew and became strong, and was filled with wisdom over time. This is the pattern of a normal human childhood: development, growth, and gradual maturation. Jesus was not born fully formed or all-knowing in His human experience — He grew up just as we do.
elements.questionHint3: Verse 40 says Jesus ’grew’ and ’became strong’ — do those words suggest something that happened all at once or over time, and what does that tell you about whether His childhood was similar to ours?

elements.text1: Tambayoyin da suka shafi halitta kan taso a duk lokacin da mutane suke tattaunawa game da ko Allah yana nan. Akwai abubuwa da yawa da za a bincika waɗanda bincike ɗaya ba zai iya rufe su ba, amma abin da ya fi muhimmanci shi ne abin da Allah da kansa yake cewa game da wannan. Don haka a wannan binciken za mu je ga bayanan Littafi Mai Tsarki — kuma yana da abubuwa da dama da za su ba ka mamaki.
elements.text3: Questions about creation come up whenever people discuss whether God exists. There is far more to explore here than one study can cover, but what matters most is what God Himself says about it. So in this study we go to the Biblical account — and it has more to say than you might expect.

elements.text1: Wane ne ya shiga cikin halitta?
elements.text3: Who was involved in the Creation?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>A sa'ad da Allah ya fara halittar Sama da duniya,

elements.question1: Farawa 1:1 da Ishaya 45:12 suna gaya mana menene game da rawar da Allah Uba ya taka a halitta?
elements.question3: What do Genesis 1:1 and Isaiah 45:12 tell us about God the Father's role in creation?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Dukansu nassosin suna gabatar da Allah a matsayin Mahallici ɗaya tilo da asalin dukan abin da ke wanzuwa. Farawa 1:1 yana faɗar wannan a sauƙaƙe da ƙarko — 'A farkon, Allah ya halicci Sama da duniya' — ba tare da barin wuri ga halitta da ta faru ba tare da shi ko kafin sa ba. Ishaya 45:12 ya ƙara taɓo kai tsaye: Allah yana magana a mutum na farko, yana cewa 'Ni ne wanda ya halicci duniya' da kuma 'hannuwana ne suka miƙa sammai'. Bai ba wani iko izinin halitta ba; ya yi ta da kansa, da cikakken iko — har ya ba da umurni ga taurari. Hoton gaba ɗaya Allah ne mai kai tsaye, mai mulki, da nufin ya halicci dukan abin da ke wanzuwa.
elements.questionHint1: A cikin Ishaya 45:12, Allah yana amfani da kalmomi 'Ni' da 'hannuwana' — me wannan harshen kai tsaye da ɗan kai ke nuna game da yadda ya shiga cikin aikin halitta, da kuma ko yana buƙatar taimakon wani?
elements.questionHint3: In Isaiah 45:12, God uses the words 'I' and 'my own hands' — what does that personal, direct language suggest about how involved He was in the act of creation, and whether He needed anyone else's help?

elements.passageText1: <sup>1</sup>Tun fil'azal akwai Kalma, Kalman nan kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman nan kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne tun fil'azal yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Dukan abubuwa sun kasance ta gare shi ne, ba kuma abin da ya kasance na abubuwan da suka kasance, sai ta game da shi.
elements.passageText2: <sup>1</sup>Tun farko akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa tare da Allah yake, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>Shi ne wanda tun farko yake tare da Allah. <sup>3</sup>Ta wurinsa ne aka yi dukan abubuwa, kuma babu wani abu a cikin dukan halittar da aka yi wanda ba tare da shi aka yi ba.
elements.passageText3: <sup>1</sup>1 Tun farar farawa akwai Kalma, Kalman kuwa yana nan tare da Allah, Kalman kuwa Allah ne. <sup>2</sup>2 Yana nan tare da Allah tun farar farawa. <sup>3</sup>3 Ta wurin Kalman ne aka halicci dukan abubuwa. In ba tare da shi ba, babu abin da aka yi wanda aka yi.
elements.passageText4: <sup>1</sup>1 Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi'eteeɗo Wolde e ɗon, e mo wondi e Allah, nden o Allah. <sup>2</sup>2 Daga ko duuniyaaru tagee bi'eteeɗo Wolde on e wondi e Allah. <sup>3</sup>3 O tagii koo ɗume. Walaa goɗɗum nder fuu ko tagaa ɗum um tagiraayi laawol maako.
elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. <sup>2</sup>The Word was with God in the beginning. <sup>3</sup>All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.

elements.passageText1: <sup>16</sup>don ta gare shi ne aka halicci dukkan kome, abubuwan da suke a sama da abubuwan da suke a ƙasa, masu ganuwa da marasa ganuwa, ko kursiyai, ko masu mulki, ko masarauta, ko masu iko, wato, dukkan abubuwa an halicce su ne ta wurinsa, shi ne kuma makomarsu. <sup>17</sup>Shi ne a gaba da dukkan abubuwa, dukkan abubuwa kuma shi ne yake riƙe da su.
elements.passageText2: <sup>16</sup>Gama ta wurinsa ne aka yi kome da kome, abubuwan da suke a sammai da kuma a nan duniya, da abubuwan da ake gani da waɗanda ba a iya gani, ko kujerun mulki, ko mulkoki, ko masu sarauta, ko ikoki. Dukan abubuwan da aka halitta, an halicce su ne ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa. <sup>17</sup>Almasihu shi ne a gaba da dukan kome, ta wurinsa ne kuma dukan halitta ta kasance a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText3: <sup>16</sup>16 Ta wurinsa aka halicci dukan abu, abubuwan da suke cikin sama da abubuwan da suke a duniya, waɗanda ake gani, da waɗanda ba a gani, ko kursiyoyi ko ikoki ko masu mulki ko hukumomi; an halicci dukan abu ta wurinsa da kuma dominsa ne. <sup>17</sup>17 Ya kasance kafin dukan abu, kuma a cikinsa ne dukan abubuwa suke a harhaɗe.
elements.passageText4: <sup>16</sup>16 Barka maako Allah tagi koo ɗume gonɗum dow e ley, ko yi'etee e ko yi'ataake, hawti e malaa'ika'en, e ginni, e koo ɗume gooduɗum laamu e bawɗe. Koo ɗume kanko tagiɗum, nden ngam maako koo ɗume tagaa. <sup>17</sup>17 Almasiihu e ɗon ko koo ɗume tagee, barka maako koo ɗume e yaha yeeso.
elements.passageText5: <sup>16</sup>For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities—all things were created through him and for him. <sup>17</sup>And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.

elements.question1: Yahaya 1:1-3 da Kolosiyawa 1:16-17 suna bayyana menene game da rawar da Yesu ya taka a halitta? Shin wannan yana canza yadda kake/kike tunaninsa?
elements.question3: What do John 1:1-3 and Colossians 1:16-17 reveal about Jesus' role in creation? Does this change how you think about Him?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yahaya 1:1-3 yana gaya mana cewa babu abin da aka halicci ba tare da Yesu ba — ba halittar da aka yi shi ba ne, sai dai shi ne wanda dukan halitta ta zo ta wurinsa. Kolosiyawa 1:16-17 yana ƙara zurfafa wannan: dukan abubuwa an halicci su ba kawai ta wurinsa ba sai dai kuma ta gare shi kuma dominsa — ma'ana dukan tsarin halitta yana da Yesu a matsayin asalinsa da manufarsa. Aya ta 17 ta ƙara da cewa shi ne 'a gaba da dukan abubuwa' kuma a cikinsa dukan abubuwa 'suke riƙe da su', ma'ana sararin samaniya yana ci gaba da wanzuwa saboda ikon ci gaba da daskarewa. Ga mutane da yawa da suka fi tunanin Yesu a matsayin malami ko mai warkarwa, wannan hoton shi ne Mahallici da Mai Kula da dukan abubuwa yana canza yadda suke fahimtar ko shi ne.
elements.questionHint1: Kolosiyawa 1:16 yana cewa an halicci dukan abubuwa ba kawai 'ta wurin' Yesu ba amma kuma 'dominsa' — menene ma'anar halitta da aka yi dominsа, kuma ta yaya wannan ke kwatanta da yadda kake/kike tunaninsa yawanci?
elements.questionHint3: Colossians 1:16 says everything was created not just 'through' Jesus but also 'for' him — what does it mean for creation to have been made for Jesus, and how does that compare with the way you normally think about Him?

elements.question1: Farawa 1:2 da Zabura 104:30 suna bayyana menene game da rawar da Ruhu Mai Tsarki ya taka a halitta? Menene ma'anar cewa dukan mutane uku na Tirniti sun shiga cikin halitta?
elements.question3: What do Genesis 1:2 and Psalm 104:30 reveal about the Holy Spirit's role in creation? What does it mean that all three persons of the Trinity were involved?

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Farawa 1:2 yana sanya Ruhun Allah a farkon farawa — yana shawagi bisa ruwayen marasa siffa, marasa kome kafin Allah ya yi magana ya kawo halitta cikin tsari. Hoton yana nuna kasancewar wanda yake shirin wanda ya shiga tun farkon farawa. Zabura 104:30 yana nuna cewa aikin halitta na Ruhu ba ya iyakance ga aikin farko kawai: sa'ad da Allah ya aika da numfashinsa (Ruhu), ana halittar halittu masu rai kuma ana sabunta ƙasa. Ruhu yana shiga cikin halitta da sabuntawa ayi, ba kawai a cikin taron farko ba. Shiga dukan mutane uku na Tirniti — Uba, Da, da Ruhu — a halitta yana gaya mana cewa halitta ba aikin ɗaya daga cikin su wanda ya yi shi kadai ba ne, sai dai aikin haɗin kai na Allah gaba ɗaya. Duniya ba ta fito daga ɓangare ɗaya na Allah ba sai dai daga cikakkiyar wanda Allah yake.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa 1:2 yana nuna Ruhu yana nan tun farkon farawa, kafin a yi kowane abu — kuma Zabura 104:30 yana nuna shi yana shiga cikin kawo sabon rayuwa. Me waɗannan lokutan biyu tare suke nuna game da faɗin shigar Ruhu cikin halitta?
elements.questionHint3: Genesis 1:2 shows the Spirit present at the very start, before anything was formed — and Psalm 104:30 shows Him involved in bringing new life into being. What do those two moments together suggest about the scope of the Spirit's involvement in creation?

elements.question1: Yana da kyau a karanta dukan sashen kafin a amsa tambayar da ke ƙasa…
elements.question: It's worth reading the whole passage before answering the question below…
elements.answer1:
1A sa'ad da Allah ya fara halittar Sama da duniya, 2duniya ba ta da siffa, sarari ce kawai, duhu kuwa yana lulluɓe da fuskar zurfin teku, Ruhun Allah kuma yana shawagi bisa ruwayen. 3Allah ya ce, "Bari haske ya kasance," sai kuwa ya kasance! 4Allah ya ga haske yana da kyau, sai Allah ya raba haske daga duhu. 5Allah ya kira hasken "yini" ya kuma kira duhu "dare." Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na ɗaya ke nan.

6Allah ya ce, "Bari sararin sama ya kasance a tsakiyar ruwayen, ya raba ruwa daga ruwa." 7Sai Allah ya yi sararin sama ya raba ruwayen da ke ƙarƙashin sararin daga ruwayen da ke sama da shi. Ya kasance haka. 8Allah ya kira sararin sama "sama." Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na biyu ke nan.

9Allah ya ce, "Bari ruwayen da ke ƙarƙashin sama su tattaru wuri ɗaya, bari ƙasar bushe ta bayyana." Ya kasance haka. 10Allah ya kira ƙasar bushe "ƙasa" ruwayen da suka tattaru kuwa ya kira "teku." Allah ya ga yana da kyau. 11Allah ya ce, "Bari ƙasa ta nuna kayan lambu: tsire-tsire masu ɗauke da iri da itatuwa masu ɗauke da 'ya'ya masu iri a cikinsu, bisa ga iri nasu." Ya kasance haka. 12Ƙasar ta nuna kayan lambu — tsire-tsire masu ɗauke da iri bisa ga iri nasu, da itatuwa masu ɗauke da 'ya'ya masu iri a cikinsu bisa ga iri nasu. Allah ya ga yana da kyau. 13Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na uku ke nan.

14Allah ya ce, "Bari haske ya kasance a cikin sararin sama don ya raba yini daga dare, bari su kasance alamomi don nuna lokuta da kwanaki da shekaru, 15bari su kuma yi hidimar haske a sararin sama don su haskaka duniya." Ya kasance haka. 16Allah ya yi hasken biyu manya — hasken mafi girma don ya yi mulki a rana da hasken ƙarami don ya yi mulki da dare. Ya kuma yi taurari. 17Allah ya sanya hasken a sararin sama don su haskaka duniya, 18don su yi mulki a rana da dare, kuma don su raba haske daga duhu. Allah ya ga yana da kyau. 19Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na huɗu ke nan.

20Allah ya ce, "Bari ruwa ya cika da rayuka masu rai, bari kuma tsuntsaye su tashi sama a bisa duniya a fadin sararin sama." 21Allah ya halicci manyan halittun teku da kowane abu mai rai mai motsi da ruwa ya cika da su bisa ga iri nasu, da kowane tsuntsaye masu fikafikan bisa ga iri nasu. Allah ya ga yana da kyau. 22Allah ya albarkace su ya ce, "Ku yi yawaita, ku ninka, ku cika ruwayen a cikin tekuna, bari tsuntsaye su ninka a bisa duniya." 23Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na biyar ke nan.

24Allah ya ce, "Bari ƙasa ta nuna halittu masu rai bisa ga iri nasu: dabbobin gida, da masu rarrafe, da namun jeji, bisa ga iri nasu." Ya kasance haka. 25Allah ya yi namun jeji bisa ga iri nasu, da dabbobin gida bisa ga iri nasu, da dukan halittu masu rarrafe a bisa ƙasa bisa ga iri nasu. Allah ya ga yana da kyau.

26Sa'an nan Allah ya ce, "Bari mu yi mutum cikin siffarmu, da kamanninmu, su mallaki kifayen da suke a cikin teku, da tsuntsayen sararin sama, da dabbobi, da dukan duniya, da kowane abu mai rarrafe da yake rarrafe bisa ƙasa." 27Haka nan fa, Allah ya halicci mutum cikin siffarsa, cikin siffar Allah ya halicci mutum, namiji da ta mace ya halicce su.

28Allah ya albarkace su ya ce musu, "Ku yi yawaita, ku ninka! Ku cika duniya, ku mallake ta! Ku yi mulki a bisa kifayen teku da tsuntsayen sama da dukan halittu da suke motsi a bisa ƙasa."

31Allah kuwa ya ga dukan abin da ya yi, ga shi kuwa yana da kyau ƙwarai. Ga maraice, ga safiya, kwana na shida ke nan.

2:1An kammala sammai da duniya da dukan abin da ke cikinsu. 2Da kwana na bakwai Allah ya gama aikin da ya kasance yana yi, sai ya huta a kwana na bakwai daga dukan aikin da ya kasance yana yi. 3Allah ya albarkaci kwana na bakwai ya keɓe shi domin a wannan kwana ya huta daga dukan aikin da ya yi a halitta.

elements.answer:
1In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. 2Now the earth was without shape and empty, and darkness was over the surface of the watery deep, but the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the water. 3God said, "Let there be light." And there was light! 4God saw that the light was good, so God separated the light from the darkness. 5God called the light "day" and the darkness "night." There was evening, and there was morning, marking the first day.

6God said, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters and let it separate water from water." 7So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. It was so. 8God called the expanse "sky." There was evening, and there was morning, a second day.

9God said, "Let the water under the sky be gathered to one place and let dry ground appear." It was so. 10God called the dry ground "land" and the gathered waters he called "seas." God saw that it was good. 11God said, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants yielding seeds and trees on the land bearing fruit with seed in it, according to their kinds." It was so. 12The land produced vegetation—plants yielding seeds according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds. God saw that it was good. 13There was evening, and there was morning, a third day.

14God said, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them be signs to indicate seasons and days and years, 15and let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth." It was so. 16God made two great lights—the greater light to rule over the day and the lesser light to rule over the night. He made the stars also. 17God placed the lights in the expanse of the sky to shine on the earth, 18to preside over the day and the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw that it was good. 19There was evening, and there was morning, a fourth day.

20God said, "Let the water swarm with swarms of living creatures and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky." 21God created the great sea creatures and every living and moving thing with which the water swarmed, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. 22God blessed them and said, "Be fruitful and multiply and fill the water in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth." 23There was evening, and there was morning, a fifth day.

24God said, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: cattle, creeping things, and wild animals, each according to their kind." It was so. 25God made the wild animals according to their kinds, the cattle according to their kinds, and all the creatures that creep along the ground according to their kinds. God saw that it was good.

26Then God said, "Let us make humankind in our image, after our likeness, so they may rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air, over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over all the creatures that move on the earth." 27God created humankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them, male and female he created them.

28God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply! Fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air and every creature that moves on the ground."

31God saw all that he had made—and it was very good! There was evening, and there was morning, the sixth day.

2:1The heavens and the earth were completed with everything that was in them. 2By the seventh day God finished the work that he had been doing, and he ceased on the seventh day all the work that he had been doing. 3God blessed the seventh day and made it holy because on it he ceased all the work that he had been doing in creation.

elements.question1: Daga Farawa 1:1–2:3, jera manyan abubuwa shida da Allah ya halitta. Wane tsari ko tsarin gini kake/kike ganin a cikin kwanakin shida?
elements.question3: From Genesis 1:1–2:3, list six main things God created. What pattern or structure do you notice across the six days?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Manyan abubuwa shida da Allah ya halitta: (1) haske da rabuwar yini da dare; (2) sararin sama da rabuwar ruwayen; (3) ƙasar bushe, tekuna, da kayan lambu; (4) rana, wata, da taurari; (5) halittun teku da tsuntsaye; (6) namun jeji da ɗan adam. Tsari a sarari yana bayyana a cikin kwanakin shida: kwanaki uku na farko sun kafa yanayi ko 'wurare' (haske, sama, ƙasa/teku), kuma kwanaki uku masu zuwa sun cika waɗannan wuraren da mazaunansu masu dace (hasken sama sun cika sama, kifi da tsuntsaye sun cika teku da sama, dabbobi da mutane sun cika ƙasa). Kowanne kwana yana ƙarewa da 'Allah ya ga yana da kyau', kuma dukan jerin yana ƙarewa da 'yana da kyau ƙwarai'. Tsarin yana tafiya daga marasa siffa kuma marasa kome zuwa tsari da cikawa — yana nuna halitta a matsayin aiki da niyya, tsari, da manufa.
elements.questionHint1: Duba kwanaki 1-3 da kwanaki 4-6 tare — ka/ki ga dangantaka tsakanin abin da aka halitta a kwana na 1 da kwana na 4, kwana na 2 da kwana na 5, kwana na 3 da kwana na 6? Me wannan haɗin yana nuna game da tsarin labarin?
elements.questionHint3: Look at days 1-3 and then days 4-6 side by side — do you notice a relationship between what is created on day 1 and day 4, day 2 and day 5, day 3 and day 6? What does that pairing suggest about the structure of the account?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Tsarin maimaita 'Allah ya ce… sai ya kasance haka' a duk Farawa 1 yana nuna cewa kalmar Allah ita kaɗai ta isa don kawo kowane abu cikin wanzuwa. Ba ya buƙatar kayan aiki, ƙoƙari, kayan aiki, ko taimako — kawai yana yi magana ne kuma gaskiyar tana amsa a nan da nan da cikakke. Wannan yana gaya mana cewa mulkin Allah a bisa halitta cikakke ne: duniyar da aka halitta ba ta wanzuwa ba tare da shi ba sai dai tana ƙarƙashin umurninsa gabaɗayanta. Babu ɗan adam, babu wani ƙarfin halitta, kuma babu kowane abu a sararin samaniya da ke da iko kwatankwacin Allah — halitta ta zo cikin wanzuwa ta kalmarsa, tana ci gaba da wanzuwa ta ikon daskarewansa, kuma tana amsa masa gabaɗayanta.
elements.questionHint1: Sa'ad da ɗan adam ya ba da umurni, ana iya biyansa ko a'a — amma sa'ad da Allah ya yi magana a Farawa 1, menene yake faruwa nan da nan koyaushe, kuma amsar nan da nan da cikakke tana gaya maka/ki menene game da irin mulkin da yake da shi?
elements.questionHint3: When a human being gives an order, it might or might not be obeyed — but when God speaks in Genesis 1, what always happens immediately, and what does that instant, total response tell you about the kind of authority He has?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bayyanar 'yana da kyau ƙwarai' yana gaya mana cewa duniya yadda Allah ya asalta ta yin ta ba ta da lahani, ba lalatattu ba, ko ta fuskacin ɗabi'a ta tsaka tsaki ba — ta dace sosai da niyyyar Allah kuma ta nuna halinsa. Babu wahala, babu mutuwa, babu ɓacin rai. Wannan yana da muhimmanci ƙwarai ga yadda muke tunanin duniya a yanzu, domin yana nuna cewa ciwo, lalacewa, da mugunta da muke gani ba fasali na asali na halitta ba ne — su baƙi ne. Duniya yadda take a yanzu ta lalace, ba haka aka tsara ta ba. Wannan yana ba mu dalili mu yi baƙin ciki game da abin da aka rasa da kuma bege na abin da Allah ya yi alkawarin mayar da shi — yana kuma nuna ba za mu yi wa Allah laifin yanayin duniya yadda take a yanzu ba.

elements.questionHint3: If God called the original creation 'very good' but the world we see today contains suffering and brokenness, what does that difference suggest about whether God designed things this way, or whether something has gone wrong since?

elements.sampleAnswer1: A cewar wannan aya, halitta tana hidimar ayyana ɗaukakar Allah da nuna aikin hannunsa. Sammai ba shaidu bebe ba ne — suna shelar wani abu game da Mahallicinsu da ƙarfi. Halitta, a wata hanya, shaida ce ta dindindin ta kowa-kowa ta wanzuwar Allah, ikonsa, da kyawunsa, da ake samu ga kowane mutum a kowane zamani da al'ada. Mutane da yawa suna gano cewa lokutan da suke cikin duniyar halitta — sararin sama da dare mai faɗi, dutsen tsaunuka, bakin teku, rikitarwar halittu masu rai — suna haifar da jin mamaki wanda yake kai da kai zuwa wanda ya wuce abin kansa zuwa wanda ya yi shi. Wannan ainihin abin da mawaƙi ya ce halitta an tsara ta don ta yi.
elements.questionHint1: Zabura tana cewa sammai suna 'shelar' wani abu — kamar dai halitta tana watsa saƙo da ƙarfi. Menene saƙon da take shelarwa, kuma taɓa samun jin wannan saƙo ka/ki sa'ad da ka/ki duba wani abu a cikin duniyar halitta?
elements.questionHint3: The psalm says the heavens 'declare' something — as if creation is actively communicating a message. What is the message it is declaring, and have you ever found yourself sensing that message when looking at something in the natural world?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Rayuwa domin ɗaukakar Allah na nufin rayuwa ta hanyar da take sa Allah ya zama mai ɗaukaka — wanda yake jan hankalin mutane zuwa ga halinsa, kyautatawarsa, da ƙimarsa maimakon zuwa ga kanmu. A aikace, ba wani aiki ɗaya mai ban mamaki ba ne sai dai tunanin rayuwa gaba ɗaya: zaɓuɓɓukan da muke yi, yadda muke ɗauke da mutane, abubuwan da muke nema da fifita, da kuma ko rayuwarmu tana kai wa wasu zuwa ga Allah ko nesa da shi. Tun da an yi mu cikin siffar Allah (Farawa 1:27), rayuwa domin ɗaukakarsa yana kuma nufin nuna halinsa — ƙaunasa, adalcinsa, amincinsa, da tausayinsa — a rayuwarmu ta yau da kullum. elements.questionHint1: Idan an halicci mu 'domin ɗaukakar Allah' ma'ana rayuwarmu an tsara ta don ta nuna zuwa gare shi, wane irin abubuwa mutum zai iya yi — ko ya guje su — a rayuwa ta yau da kullum wanda zai nuna ko ya saba da wannan manufa?

elements.sampleAnswer1: An yi cikin siffar Allah na nufin cewa ɗan adam yana nuna wani abu na yanayin Allah da musamman — hankali nsa, ma'anar ɗabi'a nsa, iyawarsa na dangantaka, ƙirƙira, da ƙauna. Babu wata halittar a Farawa da aka bayyana ta haka. An yi dabbobi 'bisa ga iri nasu', amma an yi ɗan adam bisa ga siffar Allah da kamanninsa da kansa, wanda yake sanya mu cikin rukunin daban gaba ɗaya. Yana nufin ba halittu masu rai kawai ba ne sai dai mutanen da aka tsara su don dangantaka da Allah, masu iya sanin sa, wakiltar sa a duniya, kuma ana iya ɗaukar su da alhakin ɗabi'a gare shi. Yana kuma nufin cewa kowanne rayuwar ɗan adam yana da martaba ta asali wanda ba za a cire shi ba, domin siffar Allah ce da ke cikin kowane mutum.
elements.questionHint1: Farawa yana bayyana dabbobi da aka yi 'bisa ga iri nasu' amma yana bayyana mutane daban — an yi su cikin siffar da kamanninsa na Allah da kansa. Me wannan bayani daban ke nuna game da irin dangantakar da Allah yana nufin ya yi da ɗan adam kwatankwacin sauran halitta?
elements.questionHint3: Genesis describes animals being made 'according to their kinds' but describes humans differently — made in the image and likeness of God Himself. What does that different description suggest about the kind of relationship God intends to have with human beings compared to the rest of creation?

elements.text1: Gaskiyar cewa an yi mu cikin siffar Allah yana raba ɗan adam daga sauran halitta. Yana nufin ba kawai sakamakon dama ko ilmin halitta ba ne — mu halittu ne da aka yi mu domin dangantaka da Allah, masu iya nuna halinsa a yadda muke rayuwa.
elements.text3: The fact that we are made in God's image sets human beings apart from the rest of creation. It means we are not simply the product of chance or biology — we are creatures made for relationship with God, capable of reflecting His character in the way we live.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Alhakokin biyu su ne: (1) mulkin halitta — yin mulki da sa ido a madadin Allah a matsayin wakilansa a duniya; da kuma (2) kula da ita da kiyaye ta — harshen Farawa 2:15 na ɗan lambatu ne ko mai kula, yana noma da adana abin da aka ba shi. elements.text3: Medicine and technology have given us remarkable tools to extend and improve life. But they cannot solve our deepest problem. As we have seen, every one of us has sinned, and we cannot fix that ourselves. So what do we do? In this study we look at how God calls us to respond to everything He has done for us.
Why are we unable to save ourselves from the consequences of our sin?

elements.question3: Why does Paul say that keeping the law cannot make a person right before God? What role does the law actually play, according to this verse?

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says plainly that no one will be declared righteous by observing the law — because the law’s function is not to make us righteous but to reveal that we are not. It acts as a mirror, showing us where we fall short: through the law comes the knowledge of sin. If the law’s purpose is diagnosis, not cure, then trying to use it as a path to salvation is a category error. It can tell us what is wrong; it cannot fix it.
elements.questionHint3: Paul says the law produces ’knowledge of sin’ — if that is what the law gives us, what does that tell you about whether it can also give us the solution?
